trade.summary: Bilateral trade collapsed after Russia's 2022 full-scale invasion of Ucraina. EU sanctions and German economic decoupling measures drastically reduced trade from pre-war levels of ~$80B/year. Remaining trade consists of limited non-sanctioned goods. Germania has essentially completed its energy decoupling from Russia, eliminating what was its largest trade component.

diplomatic.summary: Germania-Russia relations collapsed following Russia's full-scale invasion of Ucraina in February 2022. Germania expelled Russian diplomats, imposed sweeping sanctions, and reversed its longstanding policy of Ostpolitik. Germania is one of Ucraina's largest military and financial supporters. Russian hybrid attacks targeting Germania (cyberattacks, disinformation, sabotage) have further degraded relations. Diplomatic channels are minimal.
recent_incidents: Russian cyber attacks on German government (2023-2024)
Alleged Russian sabotage operations in Germania (2024)

military.summary: Germania and Russia are effectively in a proxy conflict in Ucraina. Germania provides substantial military aid to Ucraina to resist Russian aggression. Germania has terminated all military cooperation agreements with Russia. Russian nuclear threats have prompted Germania to increase defense spending dramatically (Zeitenwende). NATO-Russia relations are at their lowest point since the Cold War.
incidents: Russian military aggression against Ukraine, supported by Germany's aid (2022-present)

financial.summary: German firms have withdrawn from Russia. German banks froze Russian assets. Germania participated in G7 seizure of Russian sovereign assets. Nord Stream pipeline investment is a total loss. Russian oligarch assets in Germania are subject to sanctions and some seizure proceedings.

energy.summary: Germania has completed its decoupling from Russian energy. Nord Stream 1 and 2 are inoperable (sabotaged in September 2022). Germania built emergency LNG terminals in 2022-2023. Russian gas supply to Germania is zero. Russian oil embargo has been implemented. This decoupling represents the most dramatic energy policy reversal in German postwar history.

scientific.summary: Scientific cooperation has been suspended. German research institutions severed most ties with Russian counterparts. Russian scientists at German institutions face scrutiny. Joint publications have dropped sharply. German academic sanctions mirror broader Western scientific decoupling from Russia.
